description:
## Component: hx-field (NEW) **Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 4/6 libraries** Layout wrapper providing consistent label + input + help text + validation message structure. Used when wrapping non-HELiX form controls or native HTML elements in the HELiX form field pattern. ### ADR-001 Classification: Slot-Driven (Composite) Pure container — Drupal controls all content via slots. Component provides consistent field layout matching other hx-* form components. ### API Design **Properties:** `label` (string), `required` (boolean, shows indicator), `error` (string), `help-text` (string), `disabled` (visual state), `hx-size` **Slots:** Default (the form control element), `label`, `help`, `error`, `description` **CSS Parts:** `field`, `label`, `control`, `help-text`, `error-message`, `required-indicator` ### Drupal Integration ```twig {# Wrap a native Drupal form element with HELiX field layout #} {{ content.field_document_upload }} Accepted formats: PDF, DOCX, JPG (max 10MB) {# Wrap a CMS-controlled form element #}
{{ drupal_entity('block', 'custom_widget') }}
 ``` This is the "escape hatch" for Drupal — when content editors need to use native Drupal form widgets but want HELiX visual consistency. The CMS drops its own form control into the default slot, and hx-field provides the label/help/error wrapper. ### Healthcare: Wrapping legacy form widgets, custom Drupal fields, third-party integrations ### Acceptance Criteria 1. Provides consistent label/help/error layout matching all hx-* form components 2. Required indicator shown when required property set 3. Error state shows error message with correct ARIA 4. Works with ANY slotted form control (native or custom) 5. Layout matches hx-text-input, hx-select, etc. pixel-perfectly
title: T1-15: hx-field — Form Field Layout Wrapper

Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-field/hx-field.stories.ts`:
- Around line 116-909: The play functions use non-null assertions (e.g., host!,
host!.shadowRoot!, input!) which must be removed; instead add small
runtime-checked helper(s) such as getHost(canvasElement) and getShadow(host) (or
assertElement(queryResult, name)) that query the DOM, call expect(...) to assert
presence, and return the properly typed element so all play functions (e.g.,
LabelRendered.play, ErrorSuppressesHelpText.play, RequiredIndicatorPresent.play,
DisabledStateVerification.play, and others using host/input/shadowRoot) can use
the returned value without any non-null assertions; update each play to call
these helpers and use the returned typed locals.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-field/hx-field.styles.ts`:
- Around line 8-79: The styles file contains literal fallback values inside
var(...) which violates the token-only rule; remove the hardcoded fallbacks and
reference only design tokens for the affected selectors (e.g.,
:host([disabled]), .field, .field__label, .field__help-text, .field__error,
.field--error .field__label) so replace usages like var(--hx-opacity-disabled,
0.5), var(--hx-space-1, 0.25rem), var(--hx-font-family-sans, sans-serif),
var(--hx-font-size-sm, 0.875rem), var(--hx-color-neutral-700, `#343a40`),
var(--hx-color-neutral-500, `#6c757d`), var(--hx-field-error-color, `#dc3545`), and
any numeric line-height fallbacks with their token-only forms (e.g.,
var(--hx-opacity-disabled), var(--hx-space-1), var(--hx-font-family-sans),
var(--hx-font-size-sm), var(--hx-color-neutral-700),
var(--hx-color-neutral-500), var(--hx-field-error-color),
var(--hx-line-height-normal)) so the stylesheet relies solely on --hx-* tokens
and no hardcoded value

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: Line 903 uses `(host as any).required` which directly violates the project's zero-tolerance no-any policy. `HelixField` is already exported from `hx-field.js` which is already imported (side-effect). A proper type import and cast eliminates the `any`.

Changing the label color to error color in `.field--error` state is standard design system behavior (Material, Shoelace, Spectrum all do this). The feedback may flag this as overriding `--hx-field-label-color`, but applying error semantics to all error-state elements including the label is intentional and aligns with WCAG 1.4.1 (not using color alone — the error message and required indicator also convey error state). This is deliberate design, not a bug.

The test at line 259 exposes a real architectural bug: the `help` slot's `@slotchange` handler lives inside the conditionally-rendered help-text div (`${hasHelp && !hasError ? html`...` : nothing}`). When `helpText=''` and no help has been detected yet, `hasHelp` is false, the div never renders, the slot never mounts, `slotchange` never fires, and `_hasHelpSlot` is never set. This is a chicken-and-egg problem — slot-only help content is permanently invisible. The test is correct; the implementation has a structural bug.

The control wrapper has `display: contents`, meaning it creates no box and is transparent to the accessibility tree. Adding `aria-describedby` to a `display: contents` element has no effect — the attribute is on a presentational non-element in the a11y tree. Furthermore, the IDs for help/error text live in the shadow DOM, and cross-shadow-boundary `aria-describedby` references from light DOM (slotted inputs) to shadow DOM IDs are not supported by browsers. The correct approach is for consumers to wire up their form control's `aria-describedby` explicitly if needed. Adding non-functional ARIA attributes would be misleading.

Line 187 is at the end of `render()`. The same root cause as thread PRRT_kwDORRAfU85x7w4c: the conditional `${hasHelp && !hasError ? html`...` : nothing}` for the help text means the help slot never mounts when only slot content is provided (no `helpText` property). This leaves the help slot bootstrap problem unsolved and the render function structurally incorrect for the declared slot contract.
